misconception: Malolactic conversion removes acidity from the wine.
why_incorrect: MLF does not remove acid — it converts one acid (malic) to another (lactic). The total amount of acid by weight decreases only slightly because the reaction produces CO₂ as a byproduct. The perceived reduction in sharpness is due to the difference in sensory character between diprotic malic acid (sharper) and monoprotic lactic acid (softer). Saying MLF 'reduces acidity' is imprecise; saying it 'softens acidity' or 'converts malic to lactic acid, reducing perceived sharpness' is accurate.
corrected_understanding: MLF converts sharp malic acid to softer lactic acid. It makes the wine taste less sharp but does not substantially reduce the total acid content by weight.
detection_signals: after MLF the wine has no acidity

coaching_content.confusion_statement: You seem to be confusing: Malolactic conversion removes acidity from the wine.
coaching_content.evidence_statement: This is based on your answers showing this pattern.
coaching_content.improvement_signal: Correctly explaining the relationship in similar questions
